change_classes.read_only: No repo or runtime mutation; truth collection only.
change_classes.repo_only: Repo-tracked documentation, manifests, generators and SSOT updates only.
change_classes.reversible_runtime: Controlled runtime change with explicit rollback and post-verify.

lanes.label: Lane A: MVP Closeout
lanes.goal: Close the internal business MVP with the remaining visible manual proofs.
lanes.done_when: Both remaining manual blockers are passed in the latest MVP gate: device_rollout_verified and vaultwarden_recovery_material_verified.
lanes.next_operator_action: Provide the visible device rollout proof and the fresh offline Vaultwarden recovery proof.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep all other lanes visible but non-competing, then rerun the MVP gate and regenerate the AI handoff after each new proof.

lanes.label: Lane B: Website/Public Hold
lanes.goal: Keep the public website truth path and blockers visible without pulling the track into active release work.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and the public edge can be resumed with an explicit release decision.
lanes.next_operator_action: No new public release action unless a new security or reachability regression appears.
lanes.next_codex_action: Maintain the truth path, blocker list, and security posture only; do not restart CI or design work.

lanes.label: Lane C: Security/PBS/Infra
lanes.goal: Keep hardening, reapply paths, and audits green without expanding the scope into a full infrastructure program.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and the team intentionally resumes PBS and broader infra certification work.
lanes.next_operator_action: Only act if a security regression or a PBS-critical runtime issue appears.
lanes.next_codex_action: Preserve the audited hardening state and reapply paths; no new certification work before Lane A closes.

lanes.label: Lane D: Stockenweiler
lanes.goal: Keep the support concept, inventory baseline, remote-access model, and deferred phase-2 backlog visible without starting a live rollout or network marriage.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and Stockenweiler is explicitly promoted into an active support rollout.
lanes.next_operator_action: No live rollout or network marriage; only provide new facts if real devices, provider details, or FRITZ/Tailscale observations are collected.
lanes.next_codex_action: Maintain inventory, support-model documents, and the deferred phase-2 backlog; do not start site-to-site VPN, device migration, or WAN admin work.

lanes.label: Lane E: Radio/Media
lanes.goal: Keep radio and media operational without new feature work or UI expansion.
lanes.done_when: Lane A is closed and there is an explicit decision to resume radio/media expansion.
lanes.next_operator_action: Only report breakage that affects current operation.
lanes.next_codex_action: Keep the existing runtime stable and avoid scope growth.

preflight_checks: latest MVP gate still shows device_rollout_verified as pending before closure
tasks.rollback_plan: If the visible evidence is incomplete or inconsistent, keep the manual check pending and do not refresh the gate as passed.
tasks.goal: Franz Surface Laptop and iPhone are visibly accepted as released MVP devices with the required direct entry paths.
tasks.done_when: Franz Surface Laptop and Franz iPhone both have the required direct app entrypoints and the visible everyday path is confirmed.
tasks.next_operator_action: Recover the missing 2FA path blocked by the lost operator smartphone, then walk the Franz Surface Laptop and iPhone through the real direct entrypoints and capture a fresh visible acceptance result.
tasks.next_codex_action: Run scripts/device_rollout_preflight.py first. If it stays ready_for_manual_device_acceptance, use scripts/prove_device_rollout.ps1 with the fresh visible Surface Laptop and iPhone evidence so the MVP gate and AI handoff refresh automatically.

preflight_checks: Fresh visible proof exists for two separate offline recovery copies
latest MVP gate still shows vaultwarden_recovery_material_verified as pending before closure
tasks.rollback_plan: If the proof is stale or not visibly separate, keep the manual check pending and do not refresh the gate as passed.
tasks.goal: Vaultwarden recovery material exists offline in two separate physical copies.
tasks.done_when: Two separate offline copies exist and a fresh visible proof is recorded instead of relying on assumption.
tasks.next_operator_action: Create or verify two separate offline copies of the Vaultwarden recovery material and provide a fresh proof.
tasks.next_codex_action: Update the manual MVP check, rerun release_mvp_gate.py, and regenerate AI_SERVER_HANDOFF.md.

preflight_checks: Review latest public IPv6 exposure audit
Confirm Lane A is still the active delivery lane
tasks.rollback_plan: No runtime mutation; only truth-path refreshes and blocker updates.
tasks.goal: Public website work stays visible as a paused truth path, not as an active release lane.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and public edge work is intentionally reactivated.
tasks.next_operator_action: No action unless a new public regression must be documented.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the website gate, blockers, and security notes current; do not resume public release implementation.

preflight_checks: Collect current process, service and route truth with scripts/control_plane_audit.py
Confirm stock-pve remains reachable before cleaning stale local helper paths
Do not perform Windows admin-token actions without an explicit gated stop
tasks.rollback_plan: If cleanup removes a useful path, restore the canonical admin path only: direct Anker aliases plus ssh stock-pve via toolbox-backed WireGuard. Do not reactivate old parallel paths blindly.
tasks.goal: Keep one clean control plane per target, reduce stale helper paths and local noise, and make the primary admin paths explicit.
tasks.done_when: Stale local helper processes are reduced, workspace Pyrefly noise is suppressed, and the canonical admin path for Anker and Stockenweiler is verifiably documented.
tasks.next_operator_action: Only intervene if an elevated Windows token is needed for full WireGuard cleanup or if the advertised subnet route 192.168.178.0/24 for stockenweiler-pve must be approved in Tailscale admin.
tasks.next_codex_action: Run the control-plane audit, keep the workspace-level Pyrefly suppression in place, remove only clearly stale helper processes, and keep Tailscale-first with ssh stock-pve as the current Stockenweiler admin path until the bridge is approved.

preflight_checks: Confirm no new regression has appeared
tasks.rollback_plan: No broad infra mutation in watch mode; only regression response and audit refresh.
tasks.goal: Hardening, PBS status, and infrastructure guardrails remain visible without becoming the active delivery lane.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and a dedicated certification or PBS rebuild block is intentionally resumed.
tasks.next_operator_action: Only escalate if a new regression appears.
tasks.next_codex_action: Maintain the existing audit-green state and the reapply paths; avoid broad new infra work.

preflight_checks: Confirm Surface_Laptop remains modeled separately from Franz Surface Laptop
Confirm no full-tunnel or blind RouteAll requirement has been introduced
tasks.rollback_plan: Revert documentation-only drift if the live identity is disproven; do not push runtime VPN policy without a gated decision.
tasks.goal: Keep Wolfs Arbeitssurface as a separate trusted client with split access: normal local work by default, internal full access only on demand.
tasks.done_when: The device is documented and treated separately from Franz Surface Laptop, with no permanent full-tunnel and no blind RouteAll requirement.
tasks.next_operator_action: Confirm that the active WLAN device Surface_Laptop on 192.168.2.118 is Wolfs current Arbeitssurface and whether Tailscale is already installed there.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep the split-access model canonical in NETWORK_INVENTORY.md, OPERATIONS/USER_ONBOARDING_OPERATIONS.md, and AI_BOOTSTRAP_CONTEXT.md; do not fold the device into the Franz or kiosk paths.

preflight_checks: Verify stock-pve access path still works
Review latest Stockenweiler public truth, remote path and storage probe artifacts
Confirm Lane A remains active so no live rollout starts
tasks.rollback_plan: Stay in truth-collection mode only; no site marriage, no migration, no router/public changes.
tasks.goal: Stockenweiler stays visible as a prepared support track with inventory, remote-access concept, and a deferred management-first phase-2 backlog only.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and Stockenweiler is explicitly promoted into an active rollout.
tasks.next_operator_action: No live rollout, gateway work, or site marriage; only share new real device facts, provider findings, or FRITZ/Tailscale observations if available.
tasks.next_codex_action: Keep inventory, support planning, and the phase-2 backlog current; prefer read-only truth collection such as scripts/stockenweiler_pve_storage_probe.py, but do not start the rollout, site-to-site VPN, or service consolidation.

preflight_checks: Check only for breakage that affects current radio/media operation
tasks.rollback_plan: No expansion in hold mode; only restore known-good operation if breakage appears.
tasks.goal: Radio and media remain operational, but expansion work stays out of the active queue.
tasks.done_when: Lane A is closed and there is an explicit decision to expand radio/media again.
tasks.next_operator_action: Only report breakage that impacts current operation.
tasks.next_codex_action: Hold the current operating state; no new features, no new public surface.
